caveats: Demo uses synthetic visual-discovery advertising and engagement data.
The fixture is tailored for an internal executive pitch and is not reported company data.
The brief does not infer metrics outside documented dbt context.

charts.filters: All documented rows in demo fixture
charts.title: Ad revenue by monetization surface

charts.filters: All documented rows in demo fixture
charts.title: Monthly ad revenue growth

caveats: Fixture is synthetic and should not be read as reported company results.
findings.claim: Total ad revenue increased from $925M to $973M in March, but monthly growth slowed from +$69M to +$48M.

caveats: Surface attribution is based on the documented model grain: one row per month and monetization surface.
findings.claim: Brand Video Ads declined by $14M in March, offsetting a combined +$62M from Performance Shopping Ads and Visual Search Ads.

caveats: Commercial search and engaged-user fields are synthetic proxies for demo purposes.
findings.claim: Shopping-intent surfaces remained the strategic bright spot: Performance Shopping Ads added $39M and Visual Search Ads added $23M in March.

queries.sql:
select
  month,
  surface,
  net_revenue_millions,
  engaged_users_millions,
  commercial_searches_billions
from ad_revenue_by_surface
order by month, surface

with totals as (
  select
    month,
    sum(net_revenue_millions) as total_revenue_millions
  from ad_revenue_by_surface
  group by 1
),
changes as (
  select
    month,
    total_revenue_millions,
    total_revenue_millions - lag(total_revenue_millions) over (order by month) as revenue_change_millions
  from totals
)
select *
from changes
order by month

question: The CEO asks: why did ad revenue growth slow in March?
summary: Synthetic visual-discovery ad revenue still grew in March, but the growth rate slowed from +$69M in February to +$48M in March because Brand Video Ads swung from +$7M growth to a -$14M decline while shopping and visual search kept expanding.
